title: How to proceed with diagnosis of CVT electrical system failure

crossRefs.label: Diagnostic chart of hydraulic system failures by symptom
Perform initial learning of the tilt sensor.

blocks.text: How to proceed with diagnosis of CVT electrical system failure

The PGM-FI ECU, which integrates the PGM-FI control system and transmission control system, has a self-diagnostic function. If an abnormality occurs in the system input/output system while the ignition is in the ON mode, the D indicator light in the combination meter blinks at equal intervals to indicate The PGM-FI ECU has a self-diagnostic function.

1. Turn the ignition to the ON mode and confirm that the D indicator light (A) is blinking. 2. After all failure diagnostics have been completed and the necessary repair work has been performed, the end of failure diagnostics is processed.

items.text: If the D indicator light is flashing, check the Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC), Failure diagnosis table by DTC Perform fault diagnosis as directed by the

If the D indicator light is not blinking, check the DTC, and if the DTC is present Failure diagnosis table by DTC If there are no DTCs, perform fault diagnosis as indicated by the Diagnostic chart of hydraulic system failures by symptom Failure diagnosis is performed by

refs.label: Diagnostic chart of hydraulic system failures by symptom

blocks.text: 1. Connect the Honda Diagnostic System (HDS) to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat. For handling HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual. 2. Turn ignition to ON mode. 3. Verify that HDS communicates with the PGM-FI ECU. If it does not communicate, Inspect the data link circuitry. 4. Check DTC and freeze data displayed on HDS.
Turn ignition OFF (LOCK) mode.

2. Connect HDS to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat. For handling HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual. 3. Turn ignition to ON mode. 4. Verify that HDS communicates with the PGM-FI ECU. If it does not communicate, Inspect the data link circuitry. 5. Clear DTC in HDS.

The OBD Status in the Trouble Code menu can display the current diagnostic results of the DTCs that the vehicle has. This function allows the operator to enter any DTC on the screen to check if the repair has been completed. There are three types of on-screen displays of self-diagnostic results.

items.text: Normal judgment: Self-diagnosis is completed and the result is normal.
Failure determination: Self-diagnosis is completed and the result is abnormal. Repair is required.
Not completed: The self-diagnosis is not completed because it is not in the execution conditions of that DTC.

blocks.text: Turn ignition OFF (LOCK) mode.
2. Connect HDS to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat. For handling HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual.
Turn ignition to ON mode.
Verify that HDS communicates with the PGM-FI ECU. If it does not communicate, Inspect the data link circuitry.
Select AT Control Unit Reset from the HDS AT Test Mode menu.
Learning to idle. ​
Perform initial learning of the tilt sensor. ​

refs.label: Perform initial learning of the tilt sensor.

blocks.text: Turn ignition OFF (LOCK) mode.
Turn ignition to ON mode and wait for at least 30 seconds.
Turn the ignition to the OFF (LOCK) mode and disconnect the HDS from the data link coupler.

The PGM-FI ECU monitors the PGM-FI control system and rewrites system data for up to 60 minutes even after the ignition is turned to the OFF (LOCK) mode. disconnecting the PGM-FI ECU coupler while the PGM-FI ECU is monitoring the control system While the PGM-FI ECU is monitoring the control system, disconnecting the PGM-FI ECU coupler may damage the PGM-FI ECU. Therefore, the control system monitoring function of the PGM-FI ECU can be deactivated by executing the SCS short in SCS mode with HDS after the ignition is turned to the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode.
Turn ignition OFF (LOCK) mode.
2. Connect HDS to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat. For handling HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual.
Verify that HDS communicates with the PGM-FI ECU. If it does not communicate, Inspect the data link circuitry.
Execute SCS short in SCS mode with HDS.

Also check the connection status of the PGM-FI ECU coupler and the connection status of the coupler at the presumed fault location. Even in the case of a poor coupler connection, the D indicator light may blink.

6. Disconnect PGM-FI ECU couplers A (50P) and E (80P) and check the condition of the harness coupler terminals. If necessary, Remove the PGM-FI ECU. The ECU coupler part is marked with □: ECU coupler A (50P) and △: ECU coupler E (80P), respectively. When connecting ECUs, pay attention to the marks on the ECU body and ECU coupler.

7. Perform inspections according to the electrical system failure diagnosis by DTC. When using the PGM-FI ECU coupler for inspection, use a commercially available digital circuit tester (A), and for the test probe (B), use the dedicated tool pin probe male (07ZAJ-RDJA110). How to diagnose electrical system failures by DTC? Failure diagnosis table by DTC for more information.
8. When checking, insert the test probe into the inspection hole (C) of the coupler.

items: Inspect by inserting the male pin probe into the inspection hole. Do not inspect by placing the male pin probe against the coupler terminal.

blocks.text: When lifting up a vehicle to reproduce a failure, the following should be performed

items.text: Lifting the vehicle and idling the wheels may detect a DTC on the VSA; if a DTC is detected, use HDS to clear the DTC.

blocks.text: The failure detection function of some sensors, control SOL.V., and hydraulic control system uses the two consecutive detection method. When an abnormality occurs, the PGM-FI ECU stores the condition at that time as a temporary abnormality, and the PGM-FI warning light is turned on when the abnormality occurs again in the same condition after the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode and after the ignition ON mode. The information stored by the PGM-FI ECU as a temporary abnormality can be checked by the HDS (Honda Diagnostic System) as a temporary DTC*, but if it is determined to be transient under certain conditions, the stored information is automatically deleted. If the SCS terminal of the data link coupler (16P) is shorted, this function is deactivated and the PGM-FI warning light blinks immediately upon detection of an error. This facilitates failure diagnosis.
*: In systems using the two consecutive detection method, the first DTC detected is displayed as a temporary DTC.
